title: Probation Officers and Correctional Treatment Specialists
description: Provide social services to assist in rehabilitation of law offenders in custody or on probation or parole. Make recommendations for actions involving formulation of rehabilitation plan and treatment of offender, including conditional release and education and employment stipulations.

tasks.text: Prepare and maintain case folder for each assigned inmate or offender.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Gather information about offenders' backgrounds by talking to offenders, their families and friends, and other people who have relevant information.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Interview probationers and parolees regularly to evaluate their progress in accomplishing goals and maintaining the terms specified in their probation contracts and rehabilitation plans.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Discuss with offenders how such issues as drug and alcohol abuse and anger management problems might have played roles in their criminal behavior.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Supervise people on community-based sentences, such as electronically monitored home detention, and provide field supervision of probationers by conducting curfew checks or visits to home, work, or school.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Investigate alleged parole violations, using interviews, surveillance, and search and seizure.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Recommend remedial action or initiate court action in response to noncompliance with terms of probation or parole.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Arrange for medical, mental health, or substance abuse treatment services according to individual needs or court orders.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop liaisons and networks with other parole officers, community agencies, correctional institutions, psychiatric facilities, and aftercare agencies to plan for helping offenders with life adjustments.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Administer drug and alcohol tests, including random drug screens of offenders, to verify compliance with substance abuse treatment programs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Inform offenders or inmates of requirements of conditional release, such as office visits, restitution payments, or educational and employment stipulations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Participate in decisions about whether cases should go before courts and which court should hear them.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Write reports describing offenders' progress.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Conduct prehearing and presentencing investigations and testify in court regarding offenders' backgrounds and recommended sentences and sentencing conditions.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Arrange for postrelease services, such as employment, housing, counseling, education, and social activities.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide offenders or inmates with assistance in matters concerning detainers, sentences in other jurisdictions, writs, and applications for social assistance.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop and prepare packets containing information about social service agencies, assistance organizations, and programs that might be useful for inmates or offenders.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop rehabilitation programs for assigned offenders or inmates, establishing rules of conduct, goals, and objectives.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Recommend appropriate penitentiary for initial placement of an offender.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Assess the suitability of penitentiary inmates for release under parole and statutory release programs and submit recommendations to parole boards.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Identify and approve work placements for offenders with community service sentences.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
